article_meta.title: A California judge scolded members of Mark Zuckerberg's legal team after they showed up to a Los Angeles courtroom weari

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for an article that contains several instances of author-level bias. While the article is relatively restrained compared to some examples, there are defensible bias instances in the author's own prose that should have been flagged.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model returned an empty array, so there are zero attribution rule violations. No quoted text was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so there are no false positives.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Several defensible bias instances were missed: (1) 'claims that strike at the heart of the national debate over Big Tech's influence on America's youth' — Sensationalism/Emotionalism or Spin, this is author editorializing with dramatic language; (2) 'high-stakes trial' — Subjective Qualifying Adjective, author's characterization; (3) 'closely watched trial' — Subjective Qualifying Adjective; (4) The framing of the headline and lede emphasize 'scolded' which is a loaded word choice by the author. While some of these are borderline, at least 2-3 are defensible bias instances that should have been identified.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No items were flagged, so type accuracy cannot be assessed. Score 1 by default.
